metadata.title: Grade 2
domains: Number and Operations in Base Ten
metadata.notes: Auto-ingested from Common Standards Project (set 67810E9EF6944F9383DCC602A3484C23_D10003FB_grade-02). Node structure matches KnowledgeNode in knowledge_zome/integrity/src/lib.rs.

nodes.title: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, descr...
nodes.description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc., and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.

academic_standards.description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc., and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.

nodes.title: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to...
nodes.description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.

academic_standards.description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.

nodes.title: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number...
nodes.description: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces. Identify triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes.

academic_standards.description: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces. Identify triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes.

nodes.title: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a ...
nodes.description: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put-together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph.

academic_standards.description: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put-together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph.

nodes.title: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the near...
nodes.description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object. Show the measurements by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.

academic_standards.description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object. Show the measurements by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.

nodes.title: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pen...
nodes.description: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately.

academic_standards.description: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately.

nodes.title: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minute...
nodes.description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.

academic_standards.description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.

nodes.title: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equal...
nodes.description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, …, and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.

academic_standards.description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, …, and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.

nodes.title: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving leng...
nodes.description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as drawings of rulers) and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

academic_standards.description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as drawings of rulers) and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

nodes.title: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing t...
nodes.description: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length unit.

academic_standards.description: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length unit.

nodes.title: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters
nodes.description: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.

academic_standards.description: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.

nodes.title: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different length...
nodes.description: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.

academic_standards.description: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.

nodes.title: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such...
nodes.description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.

academic_standards.description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.

nodes.title: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number 100—900, and mentally subtract 10 ...
nodes.description: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number 100—900, and mentally subtract 10 or 100 from a given number 100—900.

nodes.title: Add and subtract within 1000, using concrete models or drawings and strategie...
nodes.description: Add and subtract within 1000, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method. Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.
nodes.subdomain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

academic_standards.description: Add and subtract within 1000, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method. Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.

nodes.title: Compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ...
nodes.description: Compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ones digits, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.

nodes.title: Count within 1000
nodes.description: Count within 1000; skip-count by 5s, 10s, and 100s.
nodes.subdomain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

nodes.title: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of...
nodes.description: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones; e.g., 706 equals 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones. Understand the following as special cases:

nodes.title: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arra...
nodes.description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.

academic_standards.description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.

nodes.title: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of ...
nodes.description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members, e.g., by pairing objects or counting them by 2s; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.

academic_standards.description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members, e.g., by pairing objects or counting them by 2s; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.

nodes.title: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies
nodes.description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies. By end of Grade 2, know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers.

academic_standards.description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies. By end of Grade 2, know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers.

nodes.title: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word probl...
nodes.description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

academic_standards.description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
